summary: AI-generated inference software that makes new accelerator hardware production-ready.
editor_note: A new AI chip is only useful when its inference stack catches up. Infinity's agents generate and tune kernels for specific hardware, reaching more than 90% of d-Matrix's theoretical peak in 10 hours and end-to-end Qwen3 inference in 10 days. Unlike a chipmaker or general serving framework, the bet is automated software for every accelerator, backed by a $15M Seed. The risk is whether benchmark gains repeat across architectures without each deployment becoming bespoke services.

product_evidence.claim: Infinity's official product surface says hardware specifications are the input and a model-specific, production-ready inference library is the output, with support spanning accelerator architectures and modalities.
The d-Matrix report says Infinity reached more than 90% of theoretical peak for matrix operations within 10 hours and brought Qwen3 to end-to-end inference within 10 days.
Infinity publishes a Qwen3-8B benchmark in which its generated stack delivered up to 34.3% more tokens per second than vLLM under stated identical parameters.

market_context.primary_user: AI accelerator companies, inference providers, cloud platforms, chip architects, and low-level performance engineering teams.
market_context.differentiation: Infinity generates model-aware kernels and serving libraries for each target architecture instead of asking scarce human teams to port a general framework by hand.
market_context.why_now: New accelerators are arriving faster than mature software ecosystems can form, while inference economics increasingly depend on usable throughput rather than theoretical chip specifications.

risks.claim: Infinity's published Qwen3 benchmark says its independent third-party audit is still pending.
risks.basis: The benchmark-integrity section of Infinity's official Qwen3 optimization report.

risks.claim: The falsifiable business risk is whether Infinity can repeat its results across partner architectures without each deployment requiring a large amount of bespoke engineering.
risks.basis: The seed announcement says Infinity works shoulder to shoulder with partner engineering teams and aligns revenue to performance gains.

risks.claim: The funding source does not name a formal lead; Touring Capital is recorded as the representative lead because it is the first institutional investor named.
risks.basis: VentureDex normalization under the Daily standard from Infinity's official seed announcement.
